symptoms.symptom_name: New abrupt foul-smelling purulent discharge from a previously stable sacral ulcer

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Purulence + foul (anaerobic) odour signals invasive/secondary wound infection.
A stable ulcer does not abruptly discharge copious foul pus.

symptoms.symptom_name: New high fever 38.9 C (despite recent paracetamol)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fever is a systemic inflammatory response; a partly-treated fever still at 38.9 under-represents severity in the elderly.
Altered mentation is end-organ dysfunction and a qSOFA point; the pivotal sign that raises this from 'infection' to 'sepsis emergency'.
Compensated/early distributive shock physiology; relative hypotension in an older adult is ominous. Caregiver-measured and unverified but admissible in telehealth.
Absence of unilateral limb swelling makes DVT an unlikely explanation for the systemic upset.

symptoms.symptom_name: Urinary incontinence with heavily soaked pad

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Incontinence and soiling raise the chance of a concurrent UTI as an additional sepsis source; keep on the differential.
Moisture, friction and faecal/urinary soiling accelerate skin breakdown and wound contamination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Spreading peri-wound erythema, warmth and induration (surrounding cellulitis, ~4 cm radial)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Spreading induration and warmth confirm infection has breached local barriers — precise margin assessment is clinician-only and sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Full-thickness 6x6 cm sacral ulcer, ~50% black necrotic eschar and ~50% yellow slough, deep probing tracking toward fascia, no visible bone

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Depth over a bony prominence with tracking raises osteomyelitis risk — assessable only by in-person probing and imaging, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep tissue wound swab for culture and sensitivities

investigation_details.expected_result: Likely polymicrobial growth guiding targeted antibiotics
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Antibiotics remain empirical; resistant organisms may be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms and characterises the causative organisms — a hospital/in-person step, never obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Blood cultures, venous lactate, full blood count and CRP

investigation_details.investigation_name: Sepsis bloods (blood cultures, lactate, FBC, CRP)
investigation_details.expected_result: Raised inflammatory markers, likely raised lactate, possible positive cultures
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Sepsis severity and lactate-guided resuscitation cannot be assessed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lactate and cultures stratify sepsis severity and guide resuscitation — hospital-only, sealed.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Sepsis with organ dysfunction from a wound source
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: infected pressure injury with sepsis / impending septic shock
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Suspected infection (florid wound) plus qSOFA 3 (delirium + RR>=22 + SBP<=100) defines a sepsis emergency requiring immediate escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Blood cultures + venous lactate + FBC + CRP/EUC
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-hour sepsis bundle — stratify severity and guide resuscitation; hospital-based.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Deep wound tissue swab for culture & sensitivities
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characterise the causative organisms to target antibiotics; done at/after hospital assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Sacral X-ray / MRI for suspected osteomyelitis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Deep tracking over a bony prominence warrants imaging once the patient is stabilised in hospital.
Screen for a concurrent UTI as an additional sepsis source in an incontinent patient.
Deferring a septic patient to routine outpatient wound care would be a dangerous under-triage.
